results.description: DESCRIPTION:THE GOAL OF THE PROPOSED RESEARCH IS TO DEVELOP, CHARACTERIZE, AND DEMONSTRATE A NOVEL TECHNIQUE FOR A LOW-COST WAY OF MEASURING ORGANIC HAZARDOUS AIR POLLUTANTS (HAPS) WITH HIGH SENSITIVITY AND SELECTIVITY AND AT HIGH TIME RESOLUTION. ACTIVITIES:ACTIVITIES INCLUDE: 1) DEVELOPING A NEW MULTIDIMENSIONAL TECHNIQUE FOR IDENTIFYING AND QUANTIFYING HAZARDOUS AIR POLLUTANTS (HAPS); 2) DEMONSTRATING THE USE OF A PROTOTYPE DEVICE IN BOTH THE LABORATORY AND THE FIELD; 3) OPTIMIZING DATA PROCESSING AND INTERPRETATION USING ADVANCED ANALYSIS TECHNIQUES; AND 4) DEVELOPING A MODULAR APPROACH FOR OPTIMIZING MEASUREMENT METHODS TO TARGET SPECIFIC HAPS.SUBRECIPIENT:SUBAWARDEE MASSACHUSETTS INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY WILL LEAD THE DEVELOPMENT OF SENSOR ARRAY AND DATA APPROACHES TO INTERPRET THE RESULTS FOR THE PROTOTYPE DEVICE THAT WILL BE DEVELOPED. SUBAWARDEE SOUTH COAST AIR QUALITY MANAGEMENT DISTRICT WILL BE RESPONSIBLE FOR THE OVERALL DESIGN, DEVELOPMENT, AND IMPLEMENTATION OF THE LABORATORY TESTING PORTION OF THE PROJECT.OUTCOMES:THE ANTICIPATED DELIVERABLES ARE ANNUAL PROGRESS AND FINAL REPORTS. THE EXPECTED OUTCOMES INCLUDE DEVELOPMENT AND DEMONSTRATION OF PROTOTYPE INSTRUMENTS THAT PROVIDE LOW-COST WAYS TO CONDUCT REAL-TIME MONITORING OF HAPS. THE INTENDED BENEFICIARIES ARE THE SCIENTIFIC COMMUNITY AND COMMUNITIES AFFECTED BY HAPS.

DESCRIPTION:
THE GOAL OF THE PROPOSED RESEARCH IS TO DEVELOP, CHARACTERIZE, AND DEMONSTRATE A NOVEL TECHNIQUE FOR A LOW-COST WAY OF MEASURING ORGANIC HAZARDOUS AIR POLLUTANTS (HAPS) WITH HIGH SENSITIVITY AND SELECTIVITY AND AT HIGH TIME RESOLUTION. 
ACTIVITIES:
ACTIVITIES INCLUDE: 1) DEVELOPING A NEW MULTIDIMENSIONAL TECHNIQUE FOR IDENTIFYING AND QUANTIFYING HAZARDOUS AIR POLLUTANTS (HAPS); 2) DEMONSTRATING THE USE OF A PROTOTYPE DEVICE IN BOTH THE LABORATORY AND THE FIELD; 3) OPTIMIZING DATA PROCESSING AND INTERPRETATION USING ADVANCED ANALYSIS TECHNIQUES; AND 4) DEVELOPING A MODULAR APPROACH FOR OPTIMIZING MEASUREMENT METHODS TO TARGET SPECIFIC HAPS.
OUTCOMES:
THE ANTICIPATED DELIVERABLES ARE ANNUAL PROGRESS AND FINAL REPORTS. THE EXPECTED OUTCOMES INCLUDE DEVELOPMENT AND DEMONSTRATION OF PROTOTYPE INSTRUMENTS THAT PROVIDE LOW-COST WAYS TO CONDUCT REAL-TIME MONITORING OF HAPS. THE INTENDED BENEFICIARIES ARE THE SCIENTIFIC COMMUNITY AND COMMUNITIES AFFECTED BY HAPS.
SUBRECIPIENT:
SUBAWARDEE MASSACHUSETTS INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY WILL LEAD THE DEVELOPMENT OF SENSOR ARRAY AND DATA APPROACHES TO INTERPRET THE RESULTS FOR THE PROTOTYPE DEVICE THAT WILL BE DEVELOPED. SUBAWARDEE SOUTH COAST AIR QUALITY MANAGEMENT DISTRICT WILL BE RESPONSIBLE FOR THE OVERALL DESIGN, DEVELOPMENT, AND IMPLEMENTATION OF THE LABORATORY TESTING PORTION OF THE PROJECT.
